data.sentence: Hi Paula, thanks for jumping on the call, this is Priya Patel from Aegis support — I'm the team lead on your case, case number 5889.
Yeah, hi Priya. Thanks for getting back to me. I've been waiting on this for — honestly it's been a couple of days now and we're not in a great spot.

I completely understand, and I apologize for the delay on our end. I've been reviewing the case notes and I want to make sure I fully understand what you're seeing. Can you walk me through the issue from your perspective?

Sure. So, we're in manufacturing, right, we've got a lot of OT systems, warehouse management, fleet tracking — a pretty broad attack surface. And we rely heavily on Aegis Detect to give us real-time visibility into what's happening across those environments.
Right, right.

And starting — I want to say Monday, maybe Sunday night — we started noticing that alerts are coming in with a significant lag. Like we're talking 45 minutes, an hour behind real-time. For threat monitoring that's... that's not acceptable. That's basically useless.
Yeah, I hear you, an hour of latency on threat alerts is — that's a serious problem. I'm not going to sugarcoat that.

And what's frustrating is we didn't get any notification from Aegis about a known issue or anything. I had my team spending two days thinking it was something on our side, misconfiguration, network issue — we went through all of it.
That is — yeah, I'm really sorry about that. And that's actually directly relevant to what I want to talk to you about today. So, I do need to be transparent with you about something.
Okay...

We have been dealing with an incident on our end — specifically in the Detect event processing pipeline. There was a cascading failure that started affecting event ingestion, and it resulted in significant delays across, um, a subset of our customer environments. Ridgeline is one of them.
So this is — this is an Aegis-side issue. It's not us.
Correct. This is on our side. And I want to be clear — you should have received proactive communication about this and you didn't, and that's a failure on our part. I'm genuinely sorry for that.

I mean, Priya, I — okay. I appreciate you being straight with me but this is pretty bad. We're a logistics and manufacturing company, we've got operational technology on the floor, we've got systems that if something gets through undetected because our monitoring is delayed by an hour—
Absolutely, I understand the stakes. Can I ask — during this window, do you have any secondary monitoring in place, like a SIEM or anything that's been catching events that Detect was missing?
We have a LogVault instance but it doesn't have the same coverage, especially not on the endpoint side. So no, we don't have full coverage. That's why we pay for Detect.

Understood. Okay. So let me tell you where we are from a resolution standpoint. The root cause has been identified — it was essentially a single point of failure in the event ingestion layer. Our engineering team is actively deploying a fix, which includes redundant processing nodes and a circuit breaker pattern to prevent this kind of cascading failure from happening again.
Okay. And when is that fix going out?

The fix is being rolled out now. I don't have a precise ETA to give you right this minute but we're talking hours, not days. I can commit to getting you a status update within the next two hours with a hard timeline.
Hours. Okay. I mean — and what about the events that were delayed? Are we going to get backfill on those or are those just gone?

That's a really important question and I want to give you an accurate answer rather than guess. The events are queued — they're not lost — but some of the time-sensitive context on those alerts may be degraded. I need to confirm with our engineering team exactly what the backfill situation looks like for your tenant specifically. Can I get back to you on that in the same two-hour update?

Yeah, I need that answer. Because if we've had a gap in coverage for four days and we don't know what was missed — that's something I'm going to have to report internally and potentially to our cyber insurance carrier.

Completely valid. And I'll make sure we give you the full picture so you have what you need for any internal reporting. On that note, would it be helpful if I also looped in your account manager to discuss — you know, once this is resolved — what remediation looks like from a business standpoint? Service credits, that kind of conversation.

Yes. I mean, yes, that conversation needs to happen. We're — look, I'm not trying to be difficult, I actually like the Detect product when it works. But this is a significant breach of the SLA as far as I'm concerned.

I think that's a fair characterization and I'm not going to push back on that. We'll get your account manager involved and make sure that conversation happens. In the meantime, is there anything else I can do right now — additional logging access, a direct line to our on-call engineer — anything that would help you feel more in control of the situation while we're in this window?
Actually yeah — can you set me up with a direct escalation path? Like if something happens in the next few hours and I need to reach someone, I don't want to go through the general support queue.

Absolutely. I'm going to give you my direct line right now, and I'll also send you the on-call bridge number for our Detect engineering team. You call either of those and you'll get a response in under ten minutes.
Okay. That's — that's helpful, thank you.

And Paula, I want to reiterate — this situation is not okay, and I'm not treating it as business as usual. You'll have a written update from me in two hours with the fix timeline, the backfill status, and a summary of the incident for your records. Does that work?
Yeah, that works. I'll be watching for it. And Priya — I do appreciate you being upfront about it being on your end. I've dealt with vendors who will dance around that for a week, so.
Of course. Transparency is the least we owe you right now. I'll get that email out and we'll talk again soon. Thanks for your time, Paula.
Thanks Priya. Talk soon.
